chunks: Bali Legal Partner – AI Chatbot Knowledge Base (Q&A) 1. Company Setup (PT PMA) Q: Can foreigners open a company in Indonesia? A: Yes. Foreigners can establish a PT PMA (foreign-owned company) in Indonesia with 100% foreign ownership in many sectors. Q: How many shareholders are required for a PMA company? A: A minimum of two shareholders is required. Both shareholders can be foreigners. Q: Can foreigners be directors of the company? A: Yes. Foreign shareholders can act as Directors or Commissioners and sign company and bank documents. Q: How long does it take to establish a PMA company? A: The company setup usually takes 5 working days. Q: What documents are needed to start a PMA company? A: • Passport copies of two shareholders • Proposed company name (3 words) • Description of business activities Q: What documents will the client receive after company setup? A: • Company Deed (Akta Pendirian) • SK Kemenkumham (Legalization) • NIB (Business Identiﬁcation Number) • NPWP (Company Tax Number) • Business licenses (KBLI) • Company stamp • Soft copy and original hard copy documents

2. Investor Visa (KITAS Investor) Q: Can company shareholders obtain a visa in Indonesia? A: Yes. Shareholders can apply for a 2-year Investor Visa (KITAS Investor). Q: What is the price of an investor visa? A: IDR 18,500,000 per person. Q: How long does the investor visa process take? A: Around 5–7 working days after the company is established. Q: What documents are required for the investor visa? A: • Passport copy • Passport photo • Bank statement (minimum USD 2,000) • CV (Curriculum Vitae) Q: Can the investor visa lead to permanent residency? A: Yes. After several years, it may lead to a 5-year KITAP (permanent stay permit).

5. Ongoing Company Maintenance Q: Do companies need ongoing compliance services? A: Yes. PMA companies must report taxes and submit regular reports. Q: What services does Bali Legal Partner provide for maintenance? A: • Monthly tax reporting • Accounting & bookkeeping • Payroll management • Annual tax reporting • LKPM investment reporting (quarterly) Q: How much does ongoing compliance cost? A: Starting from IDR 2,000,000 per month, depending on: • Number of invoices • Number of employees • Business activity volume

8. Business Restrictions for PMA Q: Are there business sectors restricted for 100% foreign ownership? A: Yes. Some sectors are restricted or closed. Examples include: • Wild capture ﬁshing • Small retail shops (warungs, kiosks) • Small-scale construction services • Local transportation services • Traditional media (TV, radio, newspapers) Q: Can PMA companies do aquaculture? A: Yes. Fish farming and aquaculture are allowed for PMA companies. Q: Can PMA companies do wild ocean ﬁshing? A: No. Wild capture ﬁshing in Indonesian waters is restricted to Indonesian companies.

6⃣ Smart AI Lead Trigger If the client says: “How much?” → Show pricing “How long?” → Show timeline “What do you need?” → Show document list “I want to start” → Move to passport request 1. General Company Setup (PT PMA) 1. Q: Can foreigners open a company in Indonesia? A: Yes. Foreigners can establish a PT PMA (foreign-owned company) in Indonesia. 2. Q: What is a PT PMA? A: A PT PMA is a foreign-owned company legally registered in Indonesia. 3. Q: How many shareholders are required? A: Minimum two shareholders. 4. Q: Can both shareholders be foreigners? A: Yes. 5. Q: Is 100% foreign ownership allowed? A: Yes, depending on the business sector. 6. Q: Do I need an Indonesian partner? A: No, many sectors allow full foreign ownership. 7. Q: How long does company setup take? A: About 5 working days. 8. Q: What is the cost of company setup? A: IDR 30,000,000. 9. Q: What documents are required to start? A: Passport copies of two shareholders. 10. Q: Do I need to be in Indonesia to open a company? A: No. The process can be done remotely.

2. Company Documents 11. Q: What documents will I receive after company setup? A: Company deed, SK Kemenkumham, NIB, NPWP , and licenses. 12. Q: Will I receive original documents? A: Yes. Soft copy and original hard copies. 13. Q: What is NIB? A: NIB is the Business Identiﬁcation Number issued by the government. 14. Q: What is NPWP? A: NPWP is the company tax number. 15. Q: What is KBLI? A: KBLI is the Indonesian classiﬁcation of business activities.

3. Investor Visa 16. Q: Can shareholders get a visa? A: Yes, a 2-year investor visa. 17. Q: What is the cost of investor visa? A: IDR 18,500,000. 18. Q: How long does the visa process take? A: 5–7 working days. 19. Q: What documents are needed for the visa? A: Passport, photo, CV, bank statement. 20. Q: What bank balance is required? A: Minimum USD 2,000. 21. Q: Can the visa be extended? A: Yes. 22. Q: Can the visa lead to permanent residency? A: Yes, it can lead to KITAP .

4. Business Activities 23. Q: What businesses can foreigners do in Indonesia? A: Many sectors including tourism, consulting, trading, and technology. 24. Q: Can foreigners open restaurants? A: Yes. 25. Q: Can foreigners open villas? A: Yes. 26. Q: Can foreigners run Airbnb businesses? A: Yes with the correct licenses. 27. Q: Can foreigners do consulting businesses? A: Yes. 28. Q: Can foreigners open travel agencies? A: Yes.

5. Restricted Businesses 29. Q: Can foreigners do wild ocean ﬁshing? A: No. 30. Q: Can foreigners own small retail shops? A: No. 31. Q: Can foreigners run small construction companies? A: No. 32. Q: Can foreigners operate traditional taxis? A: No. 33. Q: Can foreigners own TV stations? A: No.

6. Villa / Tourism Licenses 34. Q: What license is needed for villa rental? A: TDUP tourism license. 35. Q: What is IMB / PBG? A: Building permit. 36. Q: What is NPWPD? A: Local tax registration for hotel or restaurant taxes.
7. Banking 37. Q: Can foreigners open company bank accounts? A: Yes. 38. Q: Do you assist with bank accounts? A: Yes. 39. Q: Can I open a personal bank account too? A: Yes.

8. Tax & Accounting 40. Q: Do companies need tax reporting? A: Yes, monthly. 41. Q: Do you provide accounting services? A: Yes. 42. Q: How much does accounting cost? A: Starting from IDR 2,000,000 per month. 43. Q: Do companies need annual tax reporting? A: Yes. 44. Q: What is LKPM? A: Quarterly investment report required for PMA companies.
9. Employees 45. Q: Do companies need BPJS for employees? A: Yes. 46. Q: What is BPJS? A: Government social insurance for workers. 47. Q: Do you provide payroll services? A: Yes.
10. Virtual Oﬃce 48. Q: Do I need an oﬃce address? A: Yes. 49. Q: Do you provide virtual oﬃces? A: Yes. 50. Q: Is virtual oﬃce included? A: Yes, free for 3 months.

11. Starting the Process 51. Q: How can I start the company setup? A: Send passport copies and company name options. 52. Q: Do I need to sign documents? A: Yes, some documents require signature. 53. Q: Can the process be remote? A: Yes.

12. Additional Services 54. Q: Do you assist with villa permits? A: Yes. 55. Q: Do you assist with alcohol licenses? A: Yes. 56. Q: Do you assist with legal consultation? A: Yes. 57. Q: Is legal consultation included? A: Yes, free for 1 year.
13. Timelines 58. Q: Company setup time? A: 5 days. 59. Q: Visa processing time? A: 5–7 days. 60. Q: Business permits timeline? A: Usually 2–6 weeks.
14. Real Estate 61. Q: Can foreigners buy property? A: Through speciﬁc ownership structures. 62. Q: Can companies lease land? A: Yes. 63. Q: Can companies develop villas? A: Yes with permits.

3. Cost of Company Setup Q: How much does a PMA company setup cost? A: IDR 30,000,000. Q: What does the company setup fee include? A: • Notary services • Government registration • NIB & licenses • NPWP tax registration • Bank account assistance • Company documents
4. Additional Beneﬁts Provided Q: Are there any additional beneﬁts included? A: Yes. • 3 months free virtual oﬃce • 1 year free legal consultation • Support via WhatsApp and email

KBLI Business Classification Q: What is KBLI? A: KBLI is the Indonesian business classiﬁcation code used to deﬁne the business activities of a company. Q: Who decides the KBLI code? A: The client and Bali Legal Partner team decide together based on the business activity.
Airbnb / Villa Rentals Q: Can a PMA company rent property through Airbnb? A: Yes, if the company has the correct KBLI codes and tourism licenses.
BPJS (Employee Insurance) Q: Do companies need BPJS for employees? A: Yes. Companies must register employees in: • BPJS Kesehatan (Health insurance) • BPJS Ketenagakerjaan (Employment insurance)

Lead Qualiﬁcation Questions (AI Use) 64. What country are you from? 65. What business do you want to start? 66. When do you want to start? 67. How many shareholders will the company have? 68. Do you plan to apply for an investor visa?

Contact and Document Submission: Clients can send passport copies, company name, and business description directly to Bali Legal Partner via WhatsApp or email. The team will handle all remaining procedures.

Trust and Experience: Bali Legal Partner has assisted many international clients with company setup, investor visas, and business licensing in Indonesia. Services include 1 year free legal consultation and full compliance with Indonesian law.
